annotations.label: Commutative binary operation

anchor.snippet: A binary operation

annotations.note: CommMagma is a type with a binary operation `*` satisfying `mul_comm : ∀ a b, a * b = b * a`, the exact definition of a commutative binary operation.

annotations.label: Noncommutative operation

anchor.snippet: An operation that is not commutative is said to be

annotations.note: Mathlib has no positive class/predicate for 'noncommutative'; noncommutativity is only ever the absence of a CommMagma/CommMonoid instance.

anchors.snippet: An operation that is not commutative is said to be

annotations.label: Elements commute

anchor.snippet: One says that x commutes with y

annotations.note: `Commute a b` is defined so that `commute_iff_eq : Commute a b ↔ a * b = b * a`, exactly the notion of two elements commuting.

anchors.snippet: One says that x commutes with y

annotations.label: Commutative iff all elements commute

anchor.snippet: an operation is commutative if every two elements commute

annotations.note: `commute_iff_eq` and `mul_comm` express the per-pair equivalence, but there is no named theorem stating that an operation is a CommMagma iff every pair commutes.

annotations.label: Addition and multiplication commutative in number systems

anchor.snippet: Addition and multiplication are commutative in most number systems

annotations.note: ℕ, ℤ, ℚ, ℝ, ℂ all carry CommRing/CommMonoid instances, so `add_comm`/`mul_comm` apply to their addition and multiplication.

annotations.label: Addition commutative in vector spaces and algebras

anchor.snippet: Addition is commutative in every vector space and in every algebra

annotations.note: Module/vector-space and Algebra typeclasses require an AddCommMonoid/AddCommGroup carrier, so `add_comm` holds for their addition by construction.

annotations.label: Union and intersection commutative

anchor.snippet: Union and intersection are commutative operations on sets

annotations.note: `Set.union_comm` (`a ∪ b = b ∪ a`) and `Set.inter_comm` (`a ∩ b = b ∩ a`) state commutativity of union and intersection.

annotations.label: And and or commutative

anchor.snippet: are commutative logical operations

annotations.note: `and_comm` and `or_comm` (Lean core, used throughout Mathlib) state commutativity of logical conjunction and disjunction.

annotations.label: Division is noncommutative

anchor.snippet: Division is noncommutative, since

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem asserting the noncommutativity of division.

annotations.label: Subtraction is anti-commutative

anchor.snippet: Subtraction is noncommutative, since

annotations.note: `neg_sub : -(a - b) = b - a` (the additive form of `inv_div`) captures the anti-commutativity of subtraction, though no statement asserts subtraction is noncommutative.

annotations.label: Exponentiation is noncommutative

anchor.snippet: Exponentiation is noncommutative, since

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem asserting that exponentiation is noncommutative.

annotations.label: Some truth functions are noncommutative

anchor.snippet: Some truth functions are noncommutative

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state the noncommutativity of truth functions such as implication.

annotations.label: Function composition is noncommutative

anchor.snippet: Function composition is generally noncommutative

annotations.note: Function composition (`Function.comp`) is defined and proven associative, but its general noncommutativity is not stated.

annotations.label: Matrix multiplication is noncommutative

anchor.snippet: Matrix multiplication of square matrices

annotations.note: Square matrices form a (generally noncommutative) ring in Mathlib, but no theorem asserts that matrix multiplication is noncommutative.

annotations.label: Cross product is anti-commutative

anchor.snippet: The vector product

annotations.note: `cross_anticomm : -(v ⨯₃ w) = w ⨯₃ v` states the anti-commutativity of the cross product.

annotations.label: Commutative semigroup

anchor.snippet: a commutative semigroup is a semigroup whose operation is commutative

annotations.note: `CommSemigroup` extends `Semigroup` and `CommMagma`, i.e. a semigroup with commutative operation.

annotations.label: Commutative monoid

anchor.snippet: a commutative monoid is a monoid whose operation is commutative

annotations.note: `CommMonoid` extends `Monoid` and `CommSemigroup`, exactly a monoid with commutative multiplication.

annotations.label: Commutative group (abelian group)

anchor.snippet: a commutative group or abelian group

annotations.note: `CommGroup` extends `Group` and `CommMonoid`, the standard definition of an abelian group (additive form `AddCommGroup`).

annotations.label: Commutative ring

anchor.snippet: a commutative ring is a ring whose multiplication is commutative

annotations.note: `CommRing` extends `Ring` and `CommMonoid`, a ring whose multiplication is commutative.

annotations.label: Commutative algebra

anchor.snippet: refers only to associative algebras that have a commutative multiplication

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses a commutative algebra by combining `Algebra R A` with a `CommSemiring`/`CommRing A` instance rather than via a single dedicated `CommAlgebra` class.
